title: DB migration: hpke_keys table + save/get methods in store.rs
description:
What: Add hpke_keys SQLite table via migration and add save_hpke_key/get_hpke_key methods to relay/src/store.rs.

Algorithm:
1. Read /home/mark/WORK/nie/relay/src/store.rs to find the migration version pattern (PRAGMA user_version)
2. Find the highest current migration version N
3. Add new migration block:
   if version < (N+1) {
       conn.execute_batch("CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS hpke_keys (
           pub_id      TEXT PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL,
           public_key  BLOB NOT NULL,
           updated_at  TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
       );")?;
       conn.execute_batch("PRAGMA user_version = (N+1);")?;
   }
4. Add to Store impl:
   pub fn save_hpke_key(&self, pub_id: &str, public_key: &[u8]) -> Result<()>
     - INSERT OR REPLACE INTO hpke_keys (pub_id, public_key, updated_at) VALUES (?1, ?2, datetime('now'))
   pub fn get_hpke_key(&self, pub_id: &str) -> Result<Option<Vec<u8>>>
     - SELECT public_key FROM hpke_keys WHERE pub_id = ?1
     - Use .optional()? for None-on-missing
5. Run cargo check -p nie-relay

Defensive programming rules:
- Use PRAGMA user_version pattern (established in codebase — do NOT change existing migration blocks)
- updated_at must use datetime('now') — NOT RFC 3339 format (CLAUDE.md §9: SQLite datetime invariant)
- Never unwrap() on SQL results — return Result<_, anyhow::Error>
- Use params![] macro for all SQL bindings (no string interpolation)
- INSERT OR REPLACE handles both new and updated keys correctly
- .optional()? converts QueryReturnedNoRows to Ok(None) — do not match on error variant
acceptance_criteria: cargo check -p nie-relay exits 0; cargo test -p nie-relay passes; hpke_keys table exists in new DB; save_hpke_key/get_hpke_key round-trip works
